chunks.content:
Article: After Unit 01 gained the S2 Engine
[]: After Unit-01 gained the S2 Engine...             Quote Post by Replicator » Tue Dec 04, 2012 5:18 pm ...Could it have started walking freely without a pilot? Yui Ikari's soul is inside its core, and now Unit-01 has an S2 Engine, which would technically place it on the same level as an Angel. Or for the matter any S2 Engine-equipped EVA would fall under this category. Unit-04 before its demise, and the MP EVAs. Would those Units at least in theory, be able to operate autonomously without pilots or Dummy Plugs?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 04, 2012 6:07 pm Precisely what binds Evas to the Lilin's will, beyond obvious things like "power limits", has always been a bit steeped in vagueness. The central nervous system is subject to tampering, such that an entry plug with pilot "completes the circuit". This measure has apparently been foolproof enough to thwart even Angels (Bardiel couldn't take over Eva-03 until the entry plug with Toji was present), but hasn't worked as completely on the Evas themselves (Yui is capable of at least some movement when she has zero power and a signal terminator inserted in her spine). At any rate, my overall impression is that it takes more than an S2 Engine to liberate an Eva, otherwise the MP Evas wouldn't have made such wonderful slaves. My assumption has been that, after getting the S2 Engine, Eva-01 / Yui only retains her cybernetic implants because she will need them to make Shinji the focal point of Third Impact and HIP later. If she had really wanted to, she could have regenerated all of that mechanical crap away. (Then, this doesn't explain why she's still maintaining her implants when she's however many lightyears away from Earth and not likely to ever use them again...)

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Tue Dec 04, 2012 11:30 pm Reichu wrote: My assumption has been that, after getting the S2 Engine, Eva-01 / Yui only retains her cybernetic implants because she will need them to make Shinji the focal point of Third Impact and HIP later. If she had really wanted to, she could have regenerated all of that mechanical crap away. (Then, this doesn

Article: After Unit 01 gained the S2 Engine
ui only retains her cybernetic implants because she will need them to make Shinji the focal point of Third Impact and HIP later. If she had really wanted to, she could have regenerated all of that mechanical crap away. (Then, this doesn't explain why she's still maintaining her implants when she's however many lightyears away from Earth and not likely to ever use them again...)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 05, 2012 12:02 am pioneer wrote: Am I missing something crucial, why does she need her mechanical components for third impact and HIP? What are you referring to by 'cybernetic implants?"

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Wed Dec 05, 2012 12:52 am Reichu wrote: Most of the Eva is biological, except for a large cybernetic implant in the nape region that seems to consist mostly of: artificial vertebrae, and a large cylindrical receptacle for the entry plug that bridges the CNS and core. Yui would need to retain the entry plug system so that Shinji could fulfill his role in 3I and HIP. (She does seem to drag him into that mire somewhat purposefully, no?) As far as I'm aware, it is the dummy system personality data that's in control, as opposed to the resident soul. The implications may simply be that "animality" is at the core of all human behavior. In the case of the dummy system, the "primitive" behavior is all we get because it's simply easier to duplicate than "higher functions".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:04 am pioneer wrote: i think even against sachiel, unit 01 rips open the chest area as she did against bardiel and zereul

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:17 am Reichu wrote: Given the inevitability of Seele backlash, she was probably waiting for the most opportune moment to get an S2 Engine from an Angel. Not too soon, but not too late, either.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:28 am riffraff11235 wrote: Did Yui really have enough of her consciousness left at that point to execute that kind of plan?

[]

Article: After Unit 01 gained the S2 Engine
           Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:28 am riffraff11235 wrote: Did Yui really have enough of her consciousness left at that point to execute that kind of plan?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:34 am riffraff11235 wrote: Did Yui really have enough of her consciousness left at that point to execute that kind of plan? I always assumed that when the Evas went berserk it was mostly the raw maternal instinct of the resident soul shining through. Basically, "MUST SQUASH ANYTHING THAT TRIES TO LAY A HAND/TENTACLE/LASER ON MAH BOY!!!"

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:41 am I see what both of you are saying, and it does make a lot more sense than my original interpretations. It also makes the mechanics of the show that much more awesome! One more of my misconceptions about Eva that EGF has helped to clear up!

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Wed Dec 05, 2012 2:24 am Reichu wrote: Well, in all three cases, the core was in the chest area, so...

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Sun Dec 09, 2012 6:58 pm Reichu wrote: Basically, if you were so thoroughly dehumanized and repressed, you'd probably react to your rare moments of freedom by savagely ripping shit apart, too.
